/// Traits for converting data between Fusion and C representations.

use crate::types::*;
use std::ffi::{CStr, CString};
use fusion_core::types::classical::{ClassicalType, IntType, FloatType};
use fusion_core::FusionError;

/// Convert a Rust/Fusion type TO a C representation.
pub trait ToC {
    type Target;
    fn to_c(&self) -> Result<Self::Target, FusionError>;
}

/// Convert a C representation FROM C to a Fusion type.
/// Unsafe because C pointers might be invalid.
pub trait FromC {
    type Source;
    unsafe fn from_c(source: Self::Source) -> Result<Self, FusionError>
    where
        Self: Sized;
}

// --- Primitive Implementations ---

impl ToC for i64 {
    type Target = CInt;
    fn to_c(&self) -> Result<CInt, FusionError> {
        Ok(*self)
    }
}

impl FromC for i64 {
    type Source = CInt;
    unsafe fn from_c(source: CInt) -> Result<Self, FusionError> {
        Ok(source)
    }
}

impl ToC for bool {
    type Target = CBool;
    fn to_c(&self) -> Result<CBool, FusionError> {
        Ok(if *self { 1 } else { 0 })
    }
}

// --- String Implementation ---

impl ToC for String {
    type Target = CStringPtr;
    fn to_c(&self) -> Result<CStringPtr, FusionError> {
        let c_str = CString::new(self.as_str())
            .map_err(|_| FusionError::CompilationError("String contained null byte".into()))?;
        // Leak memory to C (caller must free via specialized Fusion API, theoretically)
        // In a real generic bridge, we'd manage lifetime better.
        Ok(c_str.into_raw() as CStringPtr)
    }
}

impl FromC for String {
    type Source = CStringPtr;
    unsafe fn from_c(source: CStringPtr) -> Result<Self, FusionError> {
        if source.is_null() {
            return Err(FusionError::CompilationError("Received NULL string ptr".into()));
        }
        let c_str = CStr::from_ptr(source);
        c_str.to_str()
            .map(|s| s.to_string())
            .map_err(|_| FusionError::CompilationError("Invalid UTF-8 in C string".into()))
    }
}
